entry.title: Docker Cheat Sheet
entry.content: Containers

entry.title: Using static site generator with Github Pages
entry.content:
• Using gh-pages branch
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage
  • Using master brunch and gh-pages branch
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage
  • Using master brunch and /docs folder
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage

There are three ways to deploy static site on Github Pages as follows. I tend to use the third scheme myself. The related Github documentation can be found here.

Using gh-pages branch

Simply deploy website content on gh-pages branch and maintain code locally.

Advantage

This is the easiest way because many static site generators have build-in deploy command. Users would only need to setup the deployment configuration once and run deploy command every time after the modification.

Disadvantage

Only website content is under the version control. The code that generates the website content is maintained locally.

Using master brunch and gh-pages branch

Deploy code on master brunch and website content on gh-pages branch.

Advantage

Both website content and code is under its own version control system.

Disadvantage

Users have to run command of both static site generator and git every time after the modification.

Using master brunch and /docs folder

Advantage

Users will only need to run command of git, and GitHub Pages will automatically read everything under the docs/ folder to publish the site.

Disadvantage

In the field of version control, users can only have the benefit of git. The version control capability of the static site generator is ignored. I personally recommend this way because the version control capability of the static site generator is not that important in most circumstances.
entry.summary:
• Using gh-pages branch
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage
  • Using master brunch and gh-pages branch
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage
  • Using master brunch and /docs folder
    • Advantage
    • Disadvantage

There are three ways to deploy static site on Github Pages as follows. I tend to use the third scheme myself. The related Github documentation can be found here.

Using gh-pages branch

Simply deploy website content on gh-pages branch and maintain code locally.

Advantage

This is the easiest way because many static site generators have build-in deploy command. Users would only need to setup the deployment configuration once and run deploy command every time after the modification.

Disadvantage

Only website content is under the version control. The code that generates the website content is maintained locally.

entry.title: Building Hexo Website with Docker
entry.content:
This is a portable and generic solution to build Hexo website with Docker.

The Hexo base image

Creating Hexo Dockerfile

Hexo Dockerfile Content

Building Hexo base image

The Apache image

Creating Apache Dockerfile

Apache Dockerfile Content

Building Apache image

Launching hexo site

Creating a Hexo container

Creating an Apache container

Resolving the Apache container’s port

Hexo website

The website can be viewed at http://localhost:32768

Updating Hexo website

Restart the Hexo container and the website will be updated.

Backing up Hexo volume
entry.summary:
This is a portable and generic solution to build Hexo website with Docker.

The Hexo base image

Creating Hexo Dockerfile

entry.title: Mac Development Config
entry.content:
• Package Manager
    • Homebrew
      • Install
      • Usage
      • Formula
        • tig
        • tree
        • readline
        • rlwrap
        • pngquant
    • Homebrew-Cask
      • Install
      • Usage
  • Terminal
    • iTerm2
    • oh-my-zsh
      • Install
      • Theme
        • agnoster
  • Text Editor
    • Atom
      • Usage
  • IDE
    • Xcode
  • Version Control
    • Git
  • Tool
    • Docker
      • Install
      • Usage

Package Manager

Homebrew

Install

Usage

brew install wget: Install formula.
brew leaves: Show installed formulae that are not dependencies of another installed formula.

Formula

tig

Tig is an ncurses-based text-mode interface for git.

tree

List contents of directories in a tree-like format.

readline

The GNU Readline library provides a set of functions for use by applications that allow users to edit command lines as they are typed in.

rlwrap

rlwrap is a ‘readline wrapper’, a small utility that uses the GNU readline library to allow the editing of keyboard input for any command.

pngquant

pngquant is a command-line utility and a library for lossy compression of PNG images.

Homebrew-Cask

Install

Usage

Terminal

iTerm2

oh-my-zsh

Install

Theme

agnoster

Text Editor

Atom

Usage

Atom Usage

IDE

Xcode

Version Control

Git

Generating a new SSH key and adding it to the ssh-agent

Tool

Docker

Install

Usage

Docker Cheat Sheet
entry.summary:
• Package Manager
    • Homebrew
      • Install
      • Usage
      • Formula
        • tig
        • tree
        • readline
        • rlwrap
        • pngquant

      • Install
      • Usage

    • iTerm2
    • oh-my-zsh

      • Theme

      • Usage

entry.title: Sorting Algorithm Analysis
entry.content:
Algorithm Worst-case running time Average-case / expected running time In-place Stable
Insertion sort1 $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes Yes
Shell sort $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes No
Selection sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes No
Heapsort $\Theta(nlgn)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ Yes No
Bubble sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes Yes
Quicksort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ (expected) Yes No
Counting sort $\Theta(k+n)$ $\Theta(k+n)$ No Yes
Radix sort2 $\Theta(d(n+k))$ $\Theta(d(n+k))$ No Yes
Bucket sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n)$ (average-case) No Yes
Merge sort $\Theta(nlgn)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ No Yes
  1. 1.n: the number of items to sort. ↩
  2. 2.For radix sort, each item is a d-digit number, where each digit takes on k possible values. ↩
entry.summary:
Algorithm Worst-case running time Average-case / expected running time In-place Stable
Insertion sort1 $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes Yes
Shell sort $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes No
Selection sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes No
Heapsort $\Theta(nlgn)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ Yes No
Bubble sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n^2)$ Yes Yes
Quicksort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ (expected) Yes No
Counting sort $\Theta(k+n)$ $\Theta(k+n)$ No Yes
Radix sort2 $\Theta(d(n+k))$ $\Theta(d(n+k))$ No Yes
Bucket sort $\Theta(n^2)$ $\Theta(n)$ (average-case) No Yes
Merge sort $\Theta(nlgn)$ $\Theta(nlgn)$ No Yes

entry.title: Atom Usage
entry.content:
• Shortcut
  • Packages
    • Tool
      • platformio-ide-terminal
      • script
      • linter
      • hydrogen
      • atom-beautify
      • split-diff
      • color-picker
      • Busy Signal
      • browser-plus
    • UI
      • minimap
      • file-icons
      • highlight-selected
      • pigments
      • qolor
      • minimap-highlight-selected
      • minimap-find-and-replace
      • minimap-pigments
      • minimap-autohide
    • Preview
      • preview
      • markdown-preview-enhanced
      • atom-html-preview
      • pdf-view
      • regex-railroad-diagram
      • hex
    • Language
      • language-markdown
      • language-babel
      • autocomplete-python
      • autocomplete-clang
      • atom-ternjs
    • Web
      • emmet
      • atom-bootstrap4
    • Git
      • gitignore-snippets
    • Coding
      • base64
      • url-encode

Shortcut

cmd-shift-L: Split selections into lines
ctrl-cmd-up, ctrl-cmd-down: Move line up or down
cmd-d: Duplicate lines
ctrl-shift-K: Delete line
cmd-K-U: Upper case the current word
cmd-K-L: Lower case the current word
cmd-D: Select the next word in the document that is the same as the currently selected word
cmd-shift-f: Search the entire project
alt-cmd-[ and alt-cmd-]: Fold or unfold the code

Packages

Tool

platformio-ide-terminal

A terminal package for Atom, complete with themes, API and more for PlatformIO IDE. Fork of terminal-plus.

script

Run code in Atom

linter

A Base Linter with Cow Powers

hydrogen

Run code and get results inline using Jupyter kernels like IPython, IJulia, and iTorch

atom-beautify

Beautify HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, Python, Ruby, Java, C, C++, C#, Objective-C, CoffeeScript, TypeScript, Coldfusion, SQL, and more in Atom

split-diff

A split pane diff tool.

color-picker

Right click or press CMD-SHIFT-C/CTRL-ALT-C to open it.

Busy Signal

A package that provides an easy to use API to show your package is performing a task.

browser-plus

Real Browser in ATOM

UI

minimap

A preview of the full source code.

file-icons

Assign file extension icons and colours for improved visual grepping

highlight-selected

Highlights the current word selected when double clicking

pigments

A package to display colors in project and files.

qolor

An atom package to color your SQL queries!

minimap-highlight-selected

A minimap binding for the highlight-selected package

minimap-find-and-replace

Minimap bindings for the find and replace package

minimap-pigments

An Atom plugin to display pigments colors in the Minimap.

minimap-autohide

Automatically hide the minimap when you aren’t using it.

Preview

preview

Ultimate previewer of source code in Atom.

markdown-preview-enhanced

The ‘BEST’ Markdown Preview you need for Atom Editor!

atom-html-preview

A live preview tool for Atom Editor.

pdf-view

Atom PDF viewer based on PDF.js

regex-railroad-diagram

Display railroad diagram of regex under cursor.

hex

View the hex dump of any file

Language

language-markdown

Adds grammar support for Markdown

language-babel

JavaScript ES201x, React JSX, Flow and GraphQL Grammar. Babel Transpiler

autocomplete-python

Python completions for packages, variables, methods, functions, with their arguments. Powered by your choice of Jedi or Kite.

autocomplete-clang

autocomplete for C/C++/ObjC using clang

atom-ternjs

JavaScript code intelligence for atom with Tern. Adds support for ES5, ES6 (JavaScript 2015), Node.js, jQuery & Angular.

Web

emmet

Emmet – the essential tool for web developers

atom-bootstrap4

Bootstrap4 Support with snippets and autocompletion.

Git

gitignore-snippets

Quickly create .gitignore files with templates from GitHub.

Coding

base64

A Base64 encoder and decoder

url-encode

URL encode or decode the selected text.
entry.summary:
• Shortcut
  • Packages
    • Tool
      • platformio-ide-terminal
      • script
      • linter
      • hydrogen
      • atom-beautify
      • split-diff
      • color-picker
      • Busy Signal
      • browser-plus
    • UI
      • minimap
      • file-icons
      • highlight-selected
      • pigments
      • qolor
      • minimap-highlight-selected
      • minimap-find-and-replace
      • minimap-pigments
      • minimap-autohide
    • Preview
      • preview
      • markdown-preview-enhanced
      • atom-html-preview
      • pdf-view
      • regex-railroad-diagram
      • hex
    • Language
      • language-markdown
      • language-babel
      • autocomplete-python
      • autocomplete-clang
      • atom-ternjs
    • Web
      • emmet
      • atom-bootstrap4
    • Git
      • gitignore-snippets
    • Coding
      • base64
      • url-encode

entry.title: Hexo Common Problems
entry.content:
• hexo server not found
  • hexo-algolia 0 posts indexed
  • hexo-math not working
  • hexo-toc generates two tocs

hexo server not found

Problem: Can’t find hexo server command.
Solution: Extra plugins line in _config.yml may cause this problem.

hexo-algolia 0 posts indexed

Problem: Run hexo algolia but no post collected.
Solution: Run hexo algolia without hexo generate. If already generated, run hexo clean to clean generated files.

hexo-math not working

Problem: MathJax is not rendered
Solution: Package hexo-math works by default. Nothing needs to be added in _config.yml file. Remove the math configuration may solve this problem.

hexo-toc generates two tocs

Problem: Two tocs are generated in one post when only one <!-- toc --> is inserted
Solution: Some themes have their own toc generator and can detect toc annotation ignoring case. Thus both hexo theme and hexo-toc will generate its own toc. The solution can be either stick to the original toc generator of the theme, or, annotate the code of toc generating in the theme and use the hexo-toc instead.
entry.summary:
• hexo server not found
  • hexo-algolia 0 posts indexed
  • hexo-math not working
  • hexo-toc generates two tocs

hexo server not found

Problem: Can’t find hexo server command.
Solution: Extra plugins line in _config.yml may cause this problem.

entry.title: Hexo Quick Guide
entry.content:
Hexo
Theme: hexo-theme-tranquilpeak

Quick deployment

With Algolia and git deployment

With Algolia and hexo deployment

Without Algolia

Create

Initializes a website.

Create a new article.

Develop

Generate static files and watch file changes.

Start a local server.

Index posts on Algolia. (This command runs without preceding hexo generate)

Deploy

Deploys your website.

Set the public_dir in _config.yml from public to docs if you want the GitHub Pages site built from the /docs folder in the master branch. More info here.
entry.summary:
Hexo
Theme: hexo-theme-tranquilpeak

Quick deployment

With Algolia and git deployment

entry.title: Remove <wbr> tags from Beautiful Soup object
entry.content:
Goal

Get plain text from a Beautiful Soup object with <br /> converted into line break and <wbr /> ignored.

Code

Result:
